topic: Root-cause synthesis of the system-spec-kit / 026 deep-review audit

noveltyJustification: First iteration for this lineage; all cluster mapping is new to the packet.

ruledOut.approach: Treat each registry row as a unique root cause
ruledOut.reason: Merged lineages contain duplicate or near-duplicate findings with different wording; row count would inflate blast radius.

focus: MCP contract drift and memory correctness
noveltyJustification: Direct source reads confirm two research questions: drift is caused by divergent hand-maintained contracts, and memory impact is real for entity-density routing but narrower for atomic-save ordering.

ruledOut.approach: Treat entity-density cache staleness as full memory corruption
ruledOut.reason: The stale value affects graph-channel routing for title/trigger terms; rows and embeddings can still update successfully.

ruledOut.approach: Treat atomic save ordering as continuous successful-save corruption
ruledOut.reason: The normal path promotes the pending file after indexing; the risk is a crash or file-persistence failure window between DB commit and final file promotion.

noveltyJustification: The iteration separated metadata drift into preserved chronology pointers, status-heuristic false completion, stale inventory snapshots, stale description renumbering, changelog rollup mismatch, and unreconciled completion metadata.

ruledOut.approach: Treat metadata drift as only isolated manual edits
ruledOut.reason: Graph status derivation and active-child pointer preservation are code-level mechanisms that can reproduce stale or misleading metadata.

ruledOut.approach: Treat graph backfill as sufficient to repair all metadata drift
ruledOut.reason: Backfill refreshes graph metadata, but active-child chronology is preserved and catalog rollups/resource maps are outside the graph metadata derivation contract.

focus: Security severity and fan-out runtime blast radius
noveltyJustification: The iteration answered the final two key questions by calibrating governed-scope defects against the local single-user threat model and separating fan-out code-path defects from current artifact proof.

ruledOut.approach: Treat P0 as unconditional for scoped retrieval and causal tools
ruledOut.reason: The research charter assumes local single-user MCP with no untrusted network input; P0 requires shared untrusted principals or remote/untrusted callers.

ruledOut.approach: Treat current eight review slices as proven concurrency under-delivery
ruledOut.reason: Their ledgers show concurrent starts from one-lineage invocations and captured stdout sentinels exist, even though summaries are not trustworthy certification artifacts.

summary: Final synthesis produced root-cause hypotheses, blast-radius estimates, severity calibration, eliminated alternatives, recommendations, and convergence report.
